pattern: Putting every value in a hash set and only starting a count from numbers that have no predecessor lets each run be measured exactly once, giving linear time despite the unsorted input.
hints: Sorting would work but costs more than necessary; membership tests can be made constant time instead.
A number begins a consecutive run only if the value one below it is absent, so you can avoid recounting the same run.
Load all values into a set; for each value with no predecessor in the set, walk upward counting consecutive members and track the longest such walk.

edge_inputs.description: scattered values contain a run of four
duplicates do not lengthen a run
single element is a run of one
all duplicates still count as length one

stress.description: dense values over a narrow band so long runs and duplicates emerge
